title: soldanella alpina var. minima
intro: Soldanella alpina var. minima, also known as Alpine Soldanella or Dwarf Alpine Soldanella, is a low-growing, perennial plant species belonging to the Primulaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and acting as an antispasmodic, making it a popular remedy in herbal medicine. Soldanella alpina var. minima can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, taken orally, or applied topically as a poultice.
